title: Argentina Private University Costs 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: How Much Does Private University Cost in Argentina?
description: Calculate annual tuition costs at Argentina's top private universities including ITBA, UTDT, and UdeSA for 2026.
intro: Compare annual tuition and enrollment fees at Argentina's leading private universities. Get accurate 2026 costs for ITBA, UTDT, UdeSA, Austral, UCA, and UCEMA across engineering, economics, law, medicine, and business programs. Costs are verified quarterly to ensure you have current information.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **monthly tuition × 12 + enrollment fee = annual cost**. Apply with real 2026 figures from Argentina's top private universities.
useCases: Parents budgeting for higher education in Argentina
Comparing costs across Argentina's top private universities
Financial planning for engineering, economics, and law programs
explanation:
## How It's Calculated

Argentina's top private universities charge tuition based on the degree program selected. The annual cost formula combines monthly tuition fees (cuotas) multiplied by 12 months plus the annual enrollment or registration fee (matrícula).

This calculator applies current 2026 rates verified across multiple universities. Costs vary significantly by institution and program, with engineering and medicine typically among the most expensive options.

## Understanding University Costs

- **Monthly tuition (cuota)**: Charged every month during the academic year
- **Enrollment fee (matrícula)**: One-time or annual registration fee, usually required at the start
- **Program duration**: Typically 4–6 years depending on the degree
- **Scholarship impact**: Merit-based scholarships can reduce costs by 20–50%

## Important Notes

This calculator provides estimated costs based on 2026 rates. Actual tuition may vary by semester and university policies. For specific financial planning, contact each university's admissions office directly. Scholarship availability and eligibility vary by institution and academic performance.

faq.q: How much does ITBA cost per year?
faq.a: ITBA charges approximately $10,000–$14,000 annually for engineering programs, depending on the specific degree and semester. Law and economics programs may have different rates.

faq.q: What's the annual tuition at UTDT?
faq.a: UTDT's annual cost ranges from $8,000–$12,000 for economics and law degrees. Exact costs depend on the program and can change each semester.

faq.q: Which Argentine private university is most affordable?
faq.a: UCA (Pontifical Catholic University) is generally one of the most affordable options at $5,000–$8,000 annually, while UdeSA and ITBA are among the most expensive at $12,000–$18,000.

faq.q: Do Argentine private universities offer scholarships?
faq.a: Yes. Most Argentine private universities offer merit-based scholarships that can provide 20–50% discounts. Each institution has its own scholarship programs and eligibility criteria.

faq.q: What's the difference between tuition and the enrollment fee?
faq.a: Tuition (cuota) is the monthly fee paid throughout the academic year. The enrollment fee (matrícula) is a separate registration fee, usually paid once per year at the start of the academic term.

faq.q: How does ITBA compare to other Argentine universities in cost?
faq.a: ITBA is among the more expensive private universities in Argentina at $10,000–$14,000 annually. UCA is more affordable at $5,000–$8,000, while UdeSA is comparable or slightly higher.
Many Argentine private universities offer payment plans or financing options. Some provide discounts for upfront annual payment. Contact your chosen university's finance office for specific options.

faq.q: How long do most degrees take at these universities?
faq.a: Most undergraduate degrees in Argentina take 4–5 years. Engineering programs may take 4–6 years depending on the specialization and university requirements.

faq.q: Are these costs in US dollars or Argentine pesos?
faq.a: The costs shown are in US dollars (USD). Current exchange rates vary, and some universities may quote prices in Argentine pesos. Check with the university for payment options.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Formula is fixed; does not require external data updates. University costs sourced from official university websites and verified quarterly.
